channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Re-examining the Meaning and Modern Relevance of People Power Beyond EDSA
item.description: The opinion piece by Vergel O. Santos reflects on the meaning and evolution of the People Power movement in the Philippines, particularly in light of its iconic 1986 EDSA uprising. The author revisits...

item.title: Dasha Burns discusses audience questions, guest appearances, and behind-the-scenes stories in Politico video segment
item.description: This Politico video segment features a compilation of interviews and discussions centered on journalist Dasha Burns, who engages with audience questions, reflects on special guest appearances, and sha...

item.title: Severe low-pressure system to bring strong winds, heavy rain and possible tornadoes to WA
item.description: A major low-pressure system is set to hit Western Australia this weekend, bringing destructive winds, heavy rainfall, and the potential for tornadoes. The Bureau of Meteorology has issued a severe wea...

item.title: AFL great Aaron Davey joins Tuggeranong Valley Indigenous Round celebrations in Canberra
item.description: A Canberra sports live blog captured a busy Saturday of action across the capital, with all four football codes featured and a notable visit from former AFL star Aaron Davey. The ex-Melbourne Demons c...

item.title: Johnnie Walker Blue Society launches in Nigeria with exclusive Lagos dinner celebrating craftsmanship and culture
item.description: The Blue Society has officially made its Nigerian debut with an exclusive and carefully curated dinner experience held in Lagos, hosted by designer Juliet Olanipekun in partnership with Johnnie Walker...

item.title: Discussion on Conditions in ICE Detention Facilities Sparks Emotional On-Air Exchange
item.description: A recent broadcast on MS NOW’s Morning Joe featured co-host Mika Brzezinski becoming visibly emotional while discussing conditions in U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) detention facilitie...

item.title: Kenya Faces Growing Concern Over Missing Children Despite Police Claims of AI Misinformation
item.description: Across Kenya, many parents are living in fear as their children go missing, with some disappearing for days or even weeks. In Kirinyaga, Cynthia Wambui’s six-year-old daughter vanished while she brief...

item.title: CBSE’s shift to on-screen marking sparks debate over implementation pace and preparedness
item.description: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE)’s introduction of on-screen marking (OSM) for Class XII board examinations has triggered a wider debate on how large-scale exam reforms should be implem...

item.title: Trump to decide on Iran ceasefire extension; calls for reopening of Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US President Donald Trump has said he will soon decide on a proposed agreement with Iran that seeks to extend an existing ceasefire between the two sides. The deal under consideration would reportedly...

item.title: Trump declared medically fit for duties but advised weight management after routine health examination
item.description: US President Donald Trump has been declared in “excellent health” following a routine medical examination conducted at Walter Reed Medical Hospital near Washington, according to a memo released by his...

item.title: Russian Intelligence Intensifies Efforts to Acquire Western Technology Amid Economic Pressure
item.description: Russian intelligence agencies have become increasingly aggressive in attempting to obtain Western technology and defence secrets, according to senior European officials. With sanctions constraining th...

item.title: Justice Department Seeks Removal of Georgia Judge From Federal Voter Registration Records Lawsuit
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) has asked for the removal of U.S. District Judge Eleanor Ross from a federal lawsuit involving access to Georgia's non-public voter registration records. The case ...

item.title: Three traditional South African bredies and their cultural significance
item.description: Bredie is a deeply rooted part of South African culinary heritage, especially valued during colder months when families turn to slow-cooked, comforting meals. The article explores how this dish, stron...

item.title: Francis Atwoli Explains How He Took His Daughter Out of Nairobi During 2024 Gen Z Protests
item.description: During the 2024 anti-Finance Bill protests in Nairobi, chaos erupted as demonstrators overwhelmed security and breached the Parliament Buildings. Several MPs had to evacuate, and part of the building ...

item.title: Residents Ordered to Evacuate in Rural Municipality of Shellbrook Due to Safety Concerns
item.description: Authorities in the Rural Municipality (RM) of Shellbrook have issued an official evacuation order for residents due to ongoing safety risks. Local officials are coordinating with emergency services to...

item.title: Blue Origin rocket explosion delays NASA lunar plans and boosts SpaceX reliance
item.description: A Blue Origin New Glenn rocket exploded on its launchpad at Cape Canaveral on Thursday evening, causing significant damage and raising concerns over the United States’ lunar ambitions. The fiery incid...

item.title: Pam Bondi shifts responsibility to Todd Blanche during House Oversight Committee hearing on Epstein files
item.description: During a recent House Oversight Committee hearing, former U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi addressed concerns regarding the handling of Epstein-related documents. Bondi claimed that the Department of J...

item.title: Damaged crane keeps 10 Penticton properties under evacuation after major construction-site fire
item.description: A major fire at a long-term care home construction site in Penticton, British Columbia, has left several residents displaced as authorities continue to manage safety risks caused by a damaged construc...

item.title: White House physician reports Trump is in excellent health following comprehensive medical examination
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has been declared to be in “excellent health” and fully fit to continue serving as commander in chief, according to a report released by his physician following a medical e...

item.title: Port-Harcourt to host 2026 NWFL Super Six finals with six top women’s teams
item.description: Port-Harcourt, Rivers State capital, don set for 2026 Nigeria Women’s Football League (NWFL) Super Six finals wey go run from May 5 to June 14. This na di first time di city go host dis top-level comp...
